text:
Once upon a time in the quaint town of Hawthorne, there lived a peculiar old man named Bartholomew Hawthorne. Bartholomew didn't keep ordinary pets. Instead, he had a talking parrot named Polly. Every morning, Polly would chirp about the latest gossip from the sparrows on the wire outside Bartholomew's window.

One serene autumn morning, Bartholomew was repairing old curtains when Polly suddenly squawked.

"Polly," he said, pausing his quest for lint balls, "we've never talked about your dreams. Tell me, bird, what do you wish to be when you grow... whatever it is?"

Polly squawked thoughtfully.

"One midnight flutter through the enchanted forest," Polly paraded like a proud peacock, "I wish to be a majestic unicorn."

Bartholomew laughed so heartily that he nearly knocked over a vase. 

"And how does a unicorn gainSuch wisdom?"
